exclusion: Tutorial sites republishing documentation without attribution.
Vendor marketing pages used as primary claims (allowed as context).
inclusion: Official documentation from major open-source projects (with version).
Peer-reviewed conference papers (NeurIPS, ICML, ACL, SIGMOD, USENIX, etc.).
Peer-reviewed journal articles (IEEE, ACM, Nature Machine Intelligence).
Preprints from arXiv / bioRxiv / SSRN with clear authorship.
Standards documents (IETF RFCs, W3C specs, ISO).
Technical blog posts from named engineers at reputable orgs.

expected_tier_distribution.rationale: Peer-reviewed primary research (conference + journal) is the foundation of novel-contribution claims.
Preprints and narrative surveys — essential for fast-moving fields where conference cycles lag.
Survey / systematic review papers for established areas.
Technical blog posts and engineering postmortems — often contain production-grade findings not published elsewhere.
Standards bodies (IETF, W3C, ISO) when the research question touches interoperability or compliance.
Conference abstracts and workshop papers — early-stage signal.

research_question: What are the current best practices for retrieval-augmented generation architectures as of 2024-2025?
